title: urbanodendron bahiense
intro: Urbanodendron bahiense, a plant species native to Brazil, is a type of tree that belongs to the Annonaceae family, commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Urbanodendron bahiense include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Urbanodendron bahiense can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies in its native region.
